title: Continuous-Time: Sensor vs. Dynamics Precision

runtime.expected_outcome: High pi_s snaps x to noisy y; high pi_f smooths over noise but lags the signal. The crossover is visible in tracking MSE.

audiences.kid: Trust your eyes more: you follow the ball snappily. Trust your brain more: you don't over-react but you lag.
audiences.real: Precision-tuning in continuous-time trades off responsiveness (pi_s) vs. smoothness (pi_f). Sweep both and pick the MSE floor.
audiences.equation: MSE is a bathtub curve in log-precision space. Minimum depends on noise level and signal frequency.
audiences.derivation: Steady-state Kalman-filter tradeoff. For a Gaussian signal with noise variance sigma^2 and signal bandwidth omega, optimal pi_s/pi_f ~ sigma^{-2} * omega^{-1} (Jazwinski 1970).

orchestrate.objective: Find the precision setting with minimum MSE on the sinusoid tracker.
orchestrate.context: sinusoid_tracker; sweep (pi_s, pi_f) in {1, 5, 20, 100} x {0.5, 1, 5}; report MSE per cell.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 8.
Jazwinski (1970), Stochastic Processes and Filtering Theory.
